summary.intent: Fix model permission errors being swallowed by error localization, and make the resulting error messages more visually prominent in the UI.
summary.outcome: Backend error messages are now preserved on the error object and displayed directly to users; error styling was changed from warning to danger color with medium font weight across three UI files.

repo: ws-connect.js calls localizeRpcError on every WS response, which maps UNAVAILABLE error codes to generic user-facing strings, losing the original server message.
Error display components in providers.js, onboarding-view.js, and page-providers.js all use CSS variable --warning for error text; --danger is the higher-visibility alternative.
The error pipeline is: Backend Err(detail) → ServiceError::Message → serialized with code: 'UNAVAILABLE' → localizeRpcError maps to generic string.

code.finding: localizeRpcError is called on every WS response; it overwrites the original server error message with a localized string based on the error code.
Error message display used text-[var(--warning)]; changed to text-[var(--danger)] with font-medium for visibility.
Same warning→danger color change applied here.
Same warning→danger color change applied here.

workflow: The fix preserved the original server message as serverMessage on the error object so consumers can prefer it over the localized fallback.
Background task notifications provide push confirmation without blocking the main conversation flow.

friction: Error localization was silently swallowing the actual backend error ('You do not have permission to access glm-5.1'), making the real cause invisible during debugging.
The issue required tracing through multiple layers (backend serialization → WS layer → error localization → UI display) before the root cause was identified.
open_items: Consider whether localizeRpcError should always preserve serverMessage as a fallback rather than requiring per-call-site handling.
Other error paths through the WS layer may have the same localization issue for different error codes beyond UNAVAILABLE.
